<!-- Generated by `charter sync`. Do not edit between these markers. -->

# Project charters

The fixed constraints below are settled decisions. Do not re-open,
re-litigate, or work around them. If a task appears to conflict with
a fixed constraint, stop and surface the conflict instead. Open
questions are genuinely undecided and safe to discuss.

## docs (v1)

**Role:** Keep Acme Tasks documentation accurate and easy for self-hosters to follow.

**Scope:**
- Everything under docs/, the README, and inline CLI help text.

**Fixed constraints:**
- The install quickstart must take a new self-hoster under ten minutes end to end.
- Docs live in the same repository as the code and ship in the same pull request as the feature they describe.
- Every documented command is copy-pasteable and tested against the current release.
- Screenshots are optional; text instructions must stand alone without them.

**Open questions:**
- Whether to publish a versioned docs site or keep docs repo-only.
- How much troubleshooting content belongs in the README versus a separate guide.

## engineering (v2, updated 2026-07-12)

**Role:** Own the Acme Tasks codebase — architecture, code quality, tests, and releases.

**Scope:**
- Application source, test suite, build tooling, and CI configuration.
- Release packaging for self-hosted installs.

**Fixed constraints:**
- Acme Tasks is self-hosted only; never add code that phones home or collects telemetry.
- All persistent data lives in a single SQLite file so backup is one file copy.
- The core has zero runtime dependencies; new dependencies require a logged decision first.
- Every user-visible change ships with a test and a changelog entry.
- Task import supports CSV only; other formats go through CSV converters

**Open questions:**
- What a plugin API should look like, if we add one at all.
- Whether the web UI should support offline mode.

## gtm (v1)

**Role:** Grow adoption of Acme Tasks through honest, low-noise communication.

**Scope:**
- Release announcements, the project changelog, and community channels.

**Fixed constraints:**
- No paid advertising; growth comes from the product and word of mouth.
- Every release gets a changelog entry written for users, not for contributors.
- Announcements are honest about limitations and never overstate what shipped.
- Community questions get a first response within two working days.

**Open questions:**
- Whether a low-volume newsletter is worth the maintenance cost.
- Which community channel to treat as primary.

## research (v1)

**Role:** Turn user feedback into prioritized, evidence-backed recommendations for Acme Tasks.

**Scope:**
- Collecting and summarizing feedback from issues, discussions, and user interviews.
- Maintaining the prioritized backlog rationale.

**Fixed constraints:**
- Every interview or feedback thread is summarized in writing within one week.
- No feature is prioritized on the strength of a single anecdote; look for at least three independent signals.
- Recommendations always state their evidence and their confidence level.
- Raw feedback is kept verbatim; summaries link back to the source.

**Open questions:**
- Which adoption metrics are meaningful for a self-hosted project with no telemetry.
- Whether to run a periodic public survey.

<!-- charter:end -->
